title: ZenRIS — All of radiology. In one browser.
description: Complete RIS + PACS platform: scheduling, diagnostic viewer, voice dictation in Romanian, teleradiology, patient portal, billing and automatic claims to CNAS (Romania's national health insurance house). On-premise or cloud, zero workstation installs.

hero.eyebrow: RIS · PACS · AI — on-premise or cloud
hero.lead_html: From scheduling to reimbursement by CNAS (Romania's national health insurance house): worklist, diagnostic viewer, AI-assisted voice dictation in Romanian, teleradiology, patient portal and billing — one platform, not a single install on any workstation.
hero.credit: A real CT study (anonymized), rendered straight from the ZenPACS archive — exactly what it looks like in the browser.

manifest.pre: A classic RIS comes with installs on every workstation, hardware dongles, counted licenses and five vendors who don't talk to each other.

story.h2_html:
The phone rings at 08:52.
By 10:13 the report is signed and the claim is ready.
story.lead: Follow a head CT through ZenRIS — from booking to payment. Every step, one module. All of it, one system.

steps.title: Booking
steps.body: The front desk sees every location, scanner and physician in real time. The patient gets confirmation and prep instructions by SMS — and if an earlier slot opens up, the waiting list offers it on its own.

steps.title: Acquisition
steps.body: By appointment time, the patient's details are already on the scanner console, via DICOM worklist. Nobody retypes anything: zero identity errors, zero lost studies.

steps.title: Arrival and triage
steps.body: Images land in the institution's archive within seconds. Routing does its job on its own: prior studies attach themselves for comparison, and AI moves urgent cases to the top of the list — before anyone even has to ask.

steps.title: Reading
steps.body: The radiologist opens the study in a tab: MPR, 3D, comparison with prior exams, hanging protocols that arrange the series exactly the way they like. In the browser — at the hospital, or from home, on call.

steps.title: Reporting
steps.body: They speak. The report writes itself, in Romanian, on the department's template. AI structures the text and suggests the conclusion; signing takes one click, and every version stays in the history.

steps.title: Delivery and reimbursement
steps.body: The patient and the referring physician get a secure link with the images and the report — no CDs, no trips back to the clinic. In the background, the services are already priced, and the CNAS claim goes out automatically: no SIUI (the legacy desktop reporting app), no exports.

story.end_after: And not one program installed on any workstation.

iso.h2_html:
One platform. Any number of institutions.
Zero data mixing.
iso.lead: ZenRIS is built for clinic chains, hospitals and teleradiology networks. Each institution lives in its own PACS archive, with its own users, templates and billing — not “logically separated” by a filter on a shared database, but separated at the level of architecture.
points_html: Its own PACS, per institution. One clinic's data has no way to end up in another — they are separate archives, not filtered rows.
Pools across boundaries. A radiologist sees exactly the studies assigned to them, from any number of clinics. The clinics never see each other. Ever.
A new institution, the same day. Its own archive, users, report letterhead, price list — without touching the rest of the platform.
iso.note_default: Click an institution: only its path lights up. Between institutions there is no path at all — the ✕ isn't decoration.
notes_abc: Clinic A sees only its own archive. The lit path is the only one that exists.
Hospital B — same rule: its archive, its users, its billing.
Center C is isolated from the other two. Not “hidden” — isolated.
iso.note_radiolog: The pool radiologist receives studies assigned from all three — but the clinics never see each other.

deploy.h2_html:
Your servers or ours.
The product is the same.
deploy.lead: There is no “cloud version” and “on-premise version” — there is one ZenRIS. The choice is about where the data lives and who runs the hardware, not about what the platform can do.
deploy.onprem_body: Data never leaves the institution. Modalities connect directly on the local network, control is total, and access is still through the browser — including from home, over a secured channel. For hospitals and groups with strict data policies.
deploy.cloud_body: Zero hardware, zero maintenance, hosting in the European Union. A new institution goes live the same day, and scaling is a decision, not an infrastructure project.
table: In the EU, in certified data centers
Your IT team, with our support
deploy.close_html: Same version. Same features. Same browser. And if you change your mind along the way, migration is a plan — not a rewrite. See the architecture in detail →

security.h2_html:
Every view, logged.
Every access, by name.
security.lead: In medical imaging, security isn't a marketing paragraph — it's a checklist. Ours is short, concrete, and verifiable at the demo.
items_html: Complete audit trail — who viewed which image and which report, when and from where; exportable for your DPO and for inspections.
Granular roles + two-factor authentication for every account.
Encryption in transit and at rest, across every component.
Automatic backup and a recovery plan that is tested, not just declared.
Per-institution isolation — the architecture described above, not a checkbox.
GDPR — legal basis, patient rights, and an access log for each patient's data.
Class I medical device, registered with ANMDMR (Romania).
security.audit_caption: excerpt from the audit log · fictitious data
audit_lines: 10:13  Dr. Ionescu M.  opened      head CT #2026-0412
10:14  Dr. Ionescu M.  signed      report v2
security.close: Ask us for the full list of controls at the demo. Or better yet — bring your IT team and let them ask.

modules.h2_html:
Ten modules. One system.
Zero integrations to build.

cards.name: ZenView
cards.text: Diagnostic viewer in the browser: MPR, 3D, comparisons, mammography. For purists, there's also ZenView Desktop — an option, not an obligation.

cards.name: ZenPACS
cards.text: Each institution's own DICOM archive: any modality connects directly, prior studies attach themselves.

cards.name: ZenReport
cards.text: Structured reports on the department's templates, electronic signing, full version history.

cards.name: ZenTelerad
cards.text: Teleradiology with pools, automatic assignment and visible deadlines — your radiology, at scale.

cards.name: ZenVoice
cards.text: Voice dictation in Romanian, native to the RIS: you speak, the report writes itself. No separate applications.

cards.name: ZenFlow
cards.text: Automation and AI triage: rule-based routing, escalations, notifications. Urgent cases don't wait.

cards.name: ZenPlan
cards.text: Multi-site scheduling: calendars per scanner and physician, SMS, waiting list.

cards.name: ZenShift
cards.text: The on-call scheduler — study routing knows who's on duty, automatically.

cards.name: ZenPortal
cards.text: Results reach the patient and the referrer through a secure link. CDs: zero.

cards.name: ZenBill · ZenAgent
cards.text: Fully automatic billing and CNAS claims — no SIUI desktop, no exports.

final.h2: See ZenRIS on your own studies.
final.lead: Book a 30-minute demo — or bring your own anonymized DICOM files and watch them cross the platform: from arrival, to report, to portal.
final.mono: The demo runs in your browser. Obviously.
